summary:
Magnesium overdose causes absence of deep tendon reflexes due to neuromuscular blockade. Calcium gluconate is recommended immediately upon recognition because it directly antagonizes magnesium’s effect on neuronal excitability and prevents respiratory depression. 

The presence of absent deep tendon reflexes indicates severe magnesium toxicity requiring urgent administration of intravenous calcium gluconate to restore neuromuscular function and prevent life-threatening complications like apnea or cardiac arrest. This is supported by multiple high-quality guidelines emphasizing prompt use of calcium as an antidote when signs of neurologic impairment occur during magnesium infusion.
